pi_items.source: Verified against setup_task.sh which creates project at this path
Verified against App.java which writes output to this exact file path
Verified against setup_task.sh and export_result.sh which both reference ProcessIrisData

Downloaded real iris.data from https://archive.ics.uci.edu/ml/machine-learning-databases/iris/iris.data, filtered by species=virginica AND petal_length>=5.0, actual count=44. Min value of 30 is reasonable lower bound.
Downloaded real iris.data, filtered by species=virginica AND petal_length>=5.0, actual count=44. Max value of 45 is tight but correctly encompasses actual result.
Verified against App.java which filters by System.getProperty('filter.species'). Iris dataset contains exactly 50 virginica records.
Verified against App.java which uses double minPetalLen = Double.parseDouble(System.getProperty('filter.min.petal.length')). Downloaded iris.data shows 44 virginica records with petal_length >= 5.0.
Independently downloaded and verified accessible from official UCI Machine Learning Repository. Dataset contains 150 records as expected.

privileged_info_summary: The task involves configuring a Java data processing application in IntelliJ IDEA to filter the publicly available UCI Iris dataset. The dataset contains 150 iris flower measurements with three species (setosa, versicolor, virginica). When filtered for virginica species with petal length >= 5.0, the dataset yields exactly 44 records, which matches the metadata bounds of 30-45. The task requires creating a Run Configuration named 'ProcessIrisData' with specific VM options (-Dfilter.species=virginica -Dfilter.min.petal.length=5.0), program arguments (data/iris.csv), and environment variables (OUTPUT_DIR=/home/ga/output, LOG_LEVEL=INFO) to process this filtered data and write results to /home/ga/output/iris_filtered.json.
